session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8642 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback for PR 8642 using the command specified in the skill:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch PR feedback and comments for PR 8642

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8642, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8642", "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from 12 erk-learn plans", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5x8Ou2", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/capability-system.md", "line": 119, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — line-range reference in prose.

Source: `src/erk/core/capabilities/registry.py:85-94`

The inline annotation `(at src/erk/core/capabilities/registry.py:85-94)` uses a line-range reference for a named Python function. Per `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md`: prefer name-based identifiers over line numbers — names survive refactoring, line numbers go stale silently.

Suggested fix: Replace the parenthetical with a prose source pointer:
```
<!-- Source: src/erk/core/capabilities/registry.py, list_optional_capabilities -->
See `list_optional_capabilities()` in `src/erk/core/capabilities/registry.py`.
```
Or simply remove the line-range annotation — the function name alone is sufficient.", "created_at": "2026-03-04T00:28:51Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5x8O1P", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/capability-system.md", "line": 125, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — enumerable catalog table will go stale.

Source: `src/erk/capabilities/skills/bundled.py:17-25`

The table listing the 5 required bundled skills is an enumerable catalog. Per `docs/learned/.claude/skills/learned-docs/learned-docs-core.md` (What Doesn't Belong): "Enumerable catalogs (error types, status values, config options) — encode as Literal types, Enums, or typed constants in source code... reference with source pointers, not tables." The `_REQUIRED_BUNDLED_SKILLS` frozenset at lines 17-25 is exactly this pattern.

As skills are added or removed from `_REQUIRED_BUNDLED_SKILLS`, this table will go stale silently.

Suggested fix: Replace the table with a source pointer:
```
<!-- Source: src/erk/capabilities/skills/bundled.py, _REQUIRED_BUNDLED_SKILLS -->
See `_REQUIRED_BUNDLED_SKILLS` in `src/erk/capabilities/skills/bundled.py` for the current list. Required skills are excluded from user-facing lists like `erk init capability list` since they are always installed.
```", "created_at": "2026-03-04T00:29:04Z" }
... [95 lines omitted] ...
